arguments.task: Open the official Salesforce Commerce developer documentation and nearby first-party pages needed to verify: a one-line product description, all documented API auth methods for Salesforce Commerce Cloud, whether access is self-serve or gated (using official pricing or access language if available), API surface type and breadth, rate limit notes, and whether an MCP server exists. Prefer canonical developer, auth, API, or pricing pages. Read the page content itself and return the exact URLs used with concise evidence notes. If some official pages are JS-rendered or inaccessible, say so explicitly.

input.thought: Launch a browser task against official Salesforce Commerce documentation to gather canonical evidence for structured research fields.

raw_result.description: Salesforce Commerce Cloud is Salesforce’s commerce platform for building B2C, B2B, and D2C shopping experiences and integrations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Commerce API authorization docs describe OAuth 2.1-based authorization and JWT access tokens for Commerce APIs, including Shopper APIs via SLAS and Admin APIs via Account Manager. No first-party evidence was gathered for API keys or Basic auth on the documented Commerce API pages reviewed.
raw_result.gating_reason: The official developer documentation reviewed describes the APIs and platform capabilities but the gathered evidence does not show any self-serve signup or public free/trial access path for Commerce Cloud itself; Salesforce Commerce Cloud is generally sold as an enterprise product, so this pass treats access as sales-gated based on the available evidence and marks for human review because explicit pricing/access language was not captured from a first-party pricing page in this run.

api_surface.breadth: Official Salesforce Commerce developer docs show a broad Commerce API surface for B2C Commerce, including shopper, admin, and related commerce guidance pages; the evidence gathered did not verify a GraphQL surface from first-party pages read in this pass.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party evidence for an official Salesforce Commerce Cloud MCP server was found in the gathered documentation. Because this field was not verified from an explicit first-party MCP page, it is marked for human review.

raw_result.blocker: Public developer docs and documented OAuth-based auth exist, but tenant/product access appears enterprise-oriented rather than clearly self-serve from the evidence gathered, so toolkit development likely requires an existing Commerce Cloud environment or manual enterprise setup.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: The gathered official Commerce API docs include a throttle-rates page and browser-extracted evidence indicating SCAPI/SLAS rate limiting, including a noted SLAS production limit of 24,000 requests per minute; exact per-endpoint limits should be confirmed in the throttle documentation for a production toolkit.
raw_result.notable_signals: Strong first-party developer documentation exists under Salesforce Developer Commerce docs, with dedicated Commerce API overview, authorization, general guidance, and throttling pages. Documentation appears somewhat JS-rendered in places, but the browser run reached the underlying canonical pages and extracted auth and throttling signals.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured uncertainty remains for access_tier and mcp_exists because this run did not capture an explicit first-party pricing/access page for Commerce Cloud nor an explicit first-party statement that no MCP exists. The auth methods, REST API type, and ready_with_workaround verdict are supported, but those two structured fields need confirmation.
